answer:
Let the pool length be \( L \) meters. The first pass at 18.5 m from the deep end implies the swimmers meet when one has swum \( 18.5 \) m and the other \( L - 18.5 \) m, so their speed ratio is \( \frac{18.5}{L - 18.5} \). The second pass at 10.5 m from the shallow end (after one rests) confirms \( L = 60 \) m.

\boxed{B}
